raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to count differing lines between two files. Use diff -y? Or diff -q? To count lines that differ: diff -y | grep -c -e '|'? Actually diff -y shows side by side with | for differences. But to count lines that differ: diff -q returns only if files differ. We can use diff -u and count lines starting with @@? Hmm simpler: diff -y --suppress-common-lines file1 file2 | wc -l. That counts lines that differ. So command: diff -y --suppress-common-lines /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | wc -l.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command to count differing lines between two files. Use diff -y? Or diff -q? To count lines that differ: diff -y | grep -c -e '|'? Actually diff -y shows side by side with | for differences. But to count lines that differ: diff -q returns only if files differ. We can use diff -u and count lines starting with @@? Hmm simpler: diff -y --suppress-common-lines file1 file2 | wc -l. That counts lines that differ. So command: diff -y --suppress-common-lines /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | wc -l.
